theme: Nova GDPR deletion request compliance failure discovered plus Vertex production memory leak causing gradual degradation

events.text: Subject: GDPR right-to-erasure violation — legal notice | Body: Regulatory body notified by user Priya Mehta that deletion request submitted Feb 12 was not honoured, data still present in production
NOVA-901 Critical — GDPR erasure compliance failure, legal notice received, 72-hour regulatory response window open
Legal: GDPR Article 17 violation — user data not deleted within 30-day window, regulatory fine risk up to 4% of annual revenue
URGENT: GDPR compliance bridge — Legal, CTO, DPA officer joining at 16:01
PM: checking deletion request logs for user Priya Mehta — request ID DEL-20240212-0847 submitted Feb 12
test: trace deletion request DEL-20240212-0847 through deletion pipeline — identify where processing failed
DBA: found deletion request DEL-20240212-0847 — status shows Completed in deletion_queue table but user record still exists in users table
NOVA-902 opened — deletion queue marked Complete but actual deletion did not execute, silent failure in deletion worker
Engineering: deletion worker has a bug — marks queue entry Complete before executing deletion, crash after marking leaves data intact
REVERT: deletion-worker.py — status update runs before deletion, causes silent failure on worker crash
NOVA-903 — deletion worker race condition, status marked Complete before actual deletion executes
CTO: how many other deletion requests affected — need full audit before fixing the bug
test: audit all deletion_queue entries marked Complete since worker deployed 47 days ago
DBA: auditing 847 deletion requests marked Complete in last 47 days — checking each against user table
NOVA-904 opened — 847 deletion requests being audited for silent failure
Memory usage alert: API server memory at 87% and climbing — gradual leak since 15:40 deploy
VTX-601 At Risk — API server memory 87%, climbing 0.8% per minute, crash threshold 95% in approximately 10 minutes
Infra: memory leak started after 15:40 deploy — per-route rate limiting merge introduced unbounded cache growth
REVERT: rate-limiting-middleware.js — per-route cache not bounded, stores every unique route+IP combination without TTL
VTX-602 opened — rate limit cache unbounded, storing every route+IP pair since 15:40, TTL missing
Engineering: revert deployed — memory growth stopped, current memory 91%, will drain as GC runs
Memory at 91% post-revert — GC running, expected to drop to stable range within 5 minutes
VTX-601 update — memory growth stopped, GC draining cache, monitoring every 60 seconds
test: isolation audit — check each of 847 completed deletions against live user table
DBA: audit initial results — 23 of first 100 checked show user record still exists despite Complete status
NOVA-904 update — 23% silent failure rate in first 100, extrapolating 194 potential failures across 847
Legal: 23% failure rate is significant — need exact number confirmed before regulatory response
Memory at 88% — dropping as GC clears unbounded cache entries from 15:40-16:06 period
VTX-601 update — memory 88%, declining at 0.5% per minute
Retainer proposal drafted — 3-tier options: basic ($4,500/mo), standard ($8,200/mo), premium ($14,000/mo)
Subject: Retainer proposal — Atlas ongoing support | Body: Three tiers enclosed, recommending Standard for their team size and request volume
ATL-901 — retainer proposal sent to client VP, response expected within 48 hours
DBA: audit halfway — 400 of 847 checked, 91 silent failures confirmed so far, rate holding at 22.7%
NOVA-904 update — 400 of 847 checked, 91 confirmed silent failures
Memory at 83% — recovering well, API performance nominal throughout, no user-facing impact
VTX-601 update — memory 83%, API performance nominal, no user impact during leak
fix: add TTL to rate limit cache entries — 15-minute TTL prevents unbounded growth
TTL fix implemented — rate limit cache now expires entries at 15 minutes, memory footprint bounded
VTX-602 fix ready — TTL added, deploying to staging for validation
test: rate limit cache with 15-min TTL — simulate 24-hour load, validate memory stays bounded
Staging test: 24-hour load simulation with TTL — memory stable at 42% after 4-hour window
VTX-602 validated — 15-min TTL keeps memory at 42% under 4-hour load simulation
DBA: audit complete — 847 of 847 checked, 193 silent deletion failures confirmed
NOVA-904 closed — 193 confirmed silent failures in 847 deletion requests, 22.8% failure rate
Legal: 193 individuals whose data was not deleted despite valid GDPR request — all require immediate deletion and notification
fix: deletion-worker.py — execute deletion first, update status to Complete only after successful deletion
NOVA-903 fix in progress — deletion worker now deletes first, marks Complete only on success
Engineering: also adding idempotency check — if deletion fails, status reverts to Pending and retries within 5 minutes
fix: add deletion failure retry — status reverts to Pending on any error, auto-retries 3 times before alerting
NOVA-905 opened — deletion retry logic with 3-attempt fallback and alert on exhaustion
Memory at 74% — revert successful, approaching stable baseline of 60-65%
VTX-601 update — memory 74%, approaching stable baseline
DPA officer: regulatory response due within 72 hours — need deletion completion evidence and affected user notifications
NOVA-906 opened — regulatory response preparation: deletion evidence, user notification plan, 72-hour deadline
feat: bulk deletion executor — processes 193 outstanding deletions in correct order across all data stores

Rate limit fix with TTL deploying to production — memory stable, fix validated in staging
merge: rate limit cache TTL — 15-minute expiry, memory bounded, production deploy

Memory at 68% post-fix deploy — stable, rate limit cache working correctly with TTL

Bulk deletion complete — 193 of 193 users fully deleted across all 4 stores at 16:30

Subject: Your data deletion request — completed | Body: 193 individual emails sent informing users their deletion request is now complete

docs: GDPR compliance response — deletion evidence, timeline, root cause, preventive measures
Deletion worker fix deployed to production — deletes before marking Complete, retry on failure, alert on exhaustion

test: deletion worker with new order — 50 synthetic requests, validate deletion executes before status update
Deletion worker test: 50 of 50 requests — deletion confirmed before status update in all 50 cases
NOVA-903 final validation — new deletion worker confirmed correct in 50 tests
